title: USD Payload Composition Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: The pinned composition, document_data_model, file_formats, and stage_population specifications are normative. This ledger covers only direct in-memory loaded external payload composition. AOUSD Core 1.0.1 treats payload inclusion as an input to composition that may be provided by stage population; bounded payload inclusion support is optional rather than a baseline compliance requirement.

status_values.implemented: In scope for the payload-namespace-source contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.optional_supported: Supported for the optional payload inclusion path described by the spec, but not required for baseline compliance.
status_values.partial: A bounded subset is included and later contracts must extend it.
status_values.deferred: Specified behavior intentionally not included in this first payload contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: The provider produces mapped direct loaded payload opinions and may expose them through a namespace-source-shaped conformance surface.
The payload opinion source consumes already-opened payload layers, not asset identifiers or resources.
Runtime payload asset opening for the optional bounded adapter payload inclusion scenario is owned by usd-composed-stage-open.
A generated target must not claim local/reference/payload LIVERPS arbitration, recursive payloads, payload load masks and mutation, or full value resolution from the payload opinion source.

obligations: Read direct external payload arcs from supplied payload arc-site records.
Use supplied already-opened payload layers keyed by authored asset identifier.
Map the target prim path and descendants under the payload-bearing prim path.
items.deferred_reason: This first provider does not resolve or open payload assets and treats direct supplied payloads as loaded.

obligations: Use the payload layer defaultPrim when an external payload has an asset but no target path.
Diagnose asset-only payloads when the payload layer has no defaultPrim.
Merge local, reference, payload, and other arc-family opinions according to LIVERPS.
Keep base/local/reference fields stronger than weaker payload target fields.
Ensure a stronger over does not erase a weaker payload def for population-facing specifier output.
items.deferred_reason: A payload opinion source must not require or merge a base/reference namespace source. Strength arbitration belongs to usd-composition-arbitrator.

obligations: Expose payload-only mapped opinions through the namespace-source boundary for conformance tests.
Allow usd-stage-population to consume that payload-only source when it is explicitly provided as the stage input.
Do not claim this source is the final composed local/reference/payload namespace source.
items.deferred_reason: Final stage population over local, reference, payload, and later arc opinions requires usd-composition-arbitrator.

obligations: Expand references authored inside loaded payload target subtrees while the referenced layers are already supplied.
Treat those nested opinions as payload-family contributions because the payload arc introduced the loaded layer stack.
Carry arc-local layerRelocates from the loaded payload layer stack onto nested reference records.
Remap child names, property names, targetPaths, and connectionPaths through the accumulated payload/reference mapping and arc-local relocate mapping.
Report missing supplied payload layers clearly.
Include the authored asset identifier in diagnostics.
Do not construct a successful namespace source when a direct required payload layer is absent.

spec_refs: aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:3935 composition input whether payload arcs are considered
aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:4261 payloads loaded or ignored by composition input
aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:5208 population mask may provide payload flag
obligations: Honor payloadInclusion=include by opening direct external payload assets and supplying a loaded-payload opinion source.
Honor payloadInclusion=omit by omitting payload asset opening and omitting the payload opinion source.
Report payload_loading=absent when payload opinions are intentionally omitted by the adapter payload inclusion scenario.
Do not diagnose authored payload arcs as missing dependencies when payloadInclusion=omit.
items.deferred_reason: Fine-grained payload load masks, unloaded-payload prim state, and load/unload mutation remain future optional payload-loading work.

spec_refs: aousd/specifications-public@2f9e746c4fbd7f48d6d2c9ac568133fe398bbfc0:core/1.0.1/core_spec.md:4268 loaded payload arcs are processed when payload inclusion is requested
obligations: Discover direct external payload asset identifiers from opened layer specs.
Resolve payload assets with the same bounded authoring-layer-relative local policy used for other opened composition dependencies.
Open payload assets through usd-layer-stack-open so payload layers may carry local sublayers.
Supply opened payload root layers to usd-payload-namespace-source keyed by authored asset identifier.

items.deferred_reason: Nested and chained payloads are not expanded by this first provider. Payload-layer payloads remain inert field data.
Path-only internal payload arcs are intentionally separate from direct external loaded payload composition.
